top_stories.title: OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund
top_stories.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed giving 5% of the company’s equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving discussions about letting the public share in the financial gains from the AI boom.
top_stories.author: Russell Brandom
top_stories.content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed giving 5% of the company’s equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving discussions about letting the public share in the financial gains from the AI boom.

top_stories.title: At ISC, JUPITER Shows What Exascale Science Looks Like
top_stories.description: JUPITER, Europe’s first exascale supercomputer at Germany’s Forschungszentrum Jülich, runs on NVIDIA Grace Hopper Superchips and NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand networking — and it’s had a busy year. As the international supercomputing community gathers at ISC in Hamburg this week, four projects running on JUPITER point to what exascale computing can actually do: map the human […]
top_stories.author: Chris Porter
top_stories.content: JUPITER, Europe’s first exascale supercomputer at Germany’s Forschungszentrum Jülich, runs on NVIDIA Grace Hopper Superchips and NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand networking — and it’s had a busy year. top_stories.title: Meet WebBrain: An Open-Source, Local-First AI Browser Agent That Reads Pages and Automates Tasks in Chrome and Firefox
top_stories.description: WebBrain is a free, MIT-licensed AI browser agent for Chrome and Firefox. It reads pages, extracts data, and automates multi-step tasks through Ask and Act modes. Run it on local models like llama.cpp or Ollama for privacy, or connect any cloud API. The post Meet WebBrain: An Open-Source, Local-First AI Browser Agent That Reads Pages and Automates Tasks in Chrome and Firefox appeared first on MarkTechPost.
top_stories.author: Asif Razzaq
top_stories.content: WebBrain is a free, MIT-licensed AI browser agent for Chrome and Firefox. It reads pages, extracts data, and automates multi-step tasks through Ask and Act modes. Run it on local models like llama.cpp or Ollama for privacy, or connect any cloud API. The post Meet WebBrain: An Open-Source, Local-First AI Browser Agent That Reads Pages and Automates Tasks in Chrome and Firefox appeared first on MarkTechPost.

top_stories.title: CUP (Common Useful Python): Building Reliable Python Workflows with Baidu’s Utility Toolkit
top_stories.description: In this tutorial, we explore CUP, Baidu's Common Useful Python library, as a practical utility toolkit for stronger Python workflows. We install it in a Colab-friendly environment and walk its subsystems step by step. We cover logging, decorators, nested configuration, caching, ID generation, thread pools, scheduling, and Linux resource monitoring. Along the way, we connect each module to real tasks like automation, concurrency, and reliability checks. The post CUP (Common Useful Python):...
top_stories.author: Sana Hassan
